message:
feat(core): port getopt short-option state machine to safe Rust (bd-go-2)

Adds crates/frankenlibc-core/src/getopt/state.rs containing the pure-safe
state machine that drives POSIX/GNU short-option scanning. The traditional
getopt ABI exposes optind/optarg/optopt globals plus an internal "next
char" pointer; this port captures all of that in a single GetoptState
value with ArgRef indirection (argv index + byte offset) so no raw
pointers cross the core boundary.

step_short(argv, optspec, &mut state) handles:
  - bundled short opts (-abc -> 'a','b','c')
  - required arg attached (-aval) and separated (-a val)
  - missing required arg returns '?' or ':' (silent-mode opterr)
  - unknown options return '?' with optopt set
  - GNU optional arg ("::") consumes only attached value
  - "--" terminator + lone "-" stop conditions

15 new unit tests in state.rs cover all the above plus ArgRef encoding
preservation; run alongside the 10 parse tests from bd-go-1 (25 total).

Sub-bead 3 (bd-go-3) will wire fl::getopt and getopt_long via the
abi shim layer to delegate to step_short, marshalling globals across
each call.
